commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Codex app-server dynamic-tool bridge and tests so unreadable tool entries, parameters, and descriptions are quarantined before registration while healthy sibling tools remain available.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +78. Total +176 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main directly reads tool entries, names, descriptions, and parameters during Codex bridge projection/spec creation; the PR adds focused tests that exercise those unreadable getter paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is protected by maintainer ownership and has no narrow automated repair blocker; the next action is human maintainer review plus rebase/validation after the linked provider-schema PR.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unreadable dynamic tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines Codex dynamic tools with unreadable array entries, unreadable `parameters`, unreadable `description`, or unsupported input schemas before Codex registration.
- Preserves healthy sibling tools so one malformed plugin-owned dynamic tool cannot poison the whole assistant turn before content.
- Emits warning and diagnostic telemetry for quarantined tools while denying calls to quarantined tools in-band.

## Landing note

This PR is intentionally pruned to the runtime quarantine only. It should land after #88880, which owns provider-safe nullable tool schema projection and the prompt snapshot shape. Keeping those snapshots out of this PR avoids duplicate fixture churn and conflicts; after #88880 lands, this branch should be rebased and rechecked.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 shard / 39 tests under Node 24.15.0
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- final diff private reported-name scrub passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: one malformed plugin-owned dynamic tool no longer prevents Codex from registering healthy dynamic tools and starting the assistant turn.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree, final head `9c95e00ca06`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: pruned speculative dynamic tool-name policy from the PR, reran focused Codex dynamic-tool tests, formatter, lint, diff check, and private-name scrub.

Evidence after fix: focused regressions pass for unsupported schemas, unreadable tool array entries, unreadable `parameters`, and unreadable `description` while preserving the healthy `message` sibling.

Observed result after fix: unreadable or unsupported dynamic tools are omitted from Codex specs, recorded in telemetry, logged/diagnosed as quarantined, and their calls return an in-band unknown/unavailable-tool response instead of crashing registration.

What was not tested: remote `pnpm check:changed` on this pruned head; current CI is expected to remain red on prompt snapshots until the branch is rebased after #88880.

## Scope note

This PR intentionally does not add new dynamic tool-name policy. Invalid names, reserved MCP prefixes, and length/character restrictions should be handled only if Codex exposes a hard contract that requires them.
target.author: vincentkoc
